title: malacothrix clevelandii var. stebbinsii
intro: Malacothrix clevelandii var. stebbinsii, also known as Cleveland's dandelion or Stebbins' dandelion, is a variety of the Malacothrix clevelandii plant, a flowering plant in the dandelion family that is native to California. This plant has been found to possess medicinal properties, particularly in its roots, which contain compounds that have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Malacothrix clevelandii var. stebbinsii include its potential use in treating digestive issues and skin problems, due to its ability to soothe inflammation and promote healing. Herbal preparations of the plant may include infusions, tinctures, and salves, which can be used to support overall health and well-being.
